definitions.text: A series of events leading to a result or product.
examples: This product of last month's quality standards committee is quite good, even though the process was flawed.

definitions.text: To perform a particular process on a thing.
To retrieve, store, classify, manipulate, transmit etc. (data, signals, etc.), especially using computer techniques.
examples: We have processed the data using our proven techniques, and have come to the following conclusions.

definitions.text: To think about a piece of information, or a concept, in order to assimilate it, and perhaps accept it in a modified state.
examples: I didn't know she had a criminal record. That will take me a while to process.

definitions.text: To walk in a procession, especially in a liturgical context.
